answer:
Authentication requirements vary depending on the specific Paygent service being used. 

For Paygentic (paygentic.io), use an API key as a Bearer token in the Authorization header [1][2]. Keys are generated in the dashboard settings and are environment-specific, starting with sk_test_ or sk_live_ [1][3].

For the Paygent backend (paygent.to), use a Bearer token (starting with pg_live_) in the Authorization header [4][5]. 

For integrations via IXOPAY or traditional Paygent API setups, authentication requires specific credentials including a Merchant ID, Connection ID, Connection Password, and sometimes client certificate details [6][7][8].

results.title: First Metering and Gating - Paygent Documentation
